{"id":"smallhold-85n","title":"Inbound Undo{Follow}: remove follower, handle unknown Follow gracefully","description":"Handle Undo{Follow} from a remote actor removing their follow of a local account. Remove from followers or follow_requests. If the row doesn't exist (perhaps already removed or never created), return 200 silently — do not 404.

**Agent workflow**: Spawn a research subagent to capture Undo{Follow} fixtures from mastodon.social and document the activity structure.

**Test discipline**:
- Test: remove existing follower → row removed from followers
- Test: Undo for non-existent follow → 200, no error
- Test: Undo for a pending follow_request → request removed
All fixtures captured from real servers. Real in-memory SQLite for DB tests — never mock the database.

**Defensive programming**:
- Verify the Undo activity actor matches the original Follow actor
- Undo.object may be the full Follow activity object or just the Follow activity URI — handle both
- No .unwrap() outside test code; all errors are Result<T, AppError>
- Validate all inputs at system boundaries","acceptance_criteria":"Existing follower removed; non-existent follow handled gracefully with 200; pending follow_request removed by Undo; both object shapes handled","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:28:38Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:28:38Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-ms0","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:28:41Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
